message:
fix(trace): make TraceMetadata deterministic by defaulting recorded_at to 0

TraceMetadata::new() previously called SystemTime::now() to populate
recorded_at, meaning two LabRuntime instances created with the same
seed would produce traces with different metadata, breaking the
deterministic replay guarantee that identical seeds yield identical
executions.

Changes:
- TraceMetadata::new(): recorded_at now defaults to 0 instead of
  capturing wall-clock time. This makes metadata a pure function of
  the seed, ensuring identical seeds produce byte-identical traces.
- TraceMetadata::recorded_at doc comment: updated to document that 0
  means "no wall-clock timestamp attached" and that deterministic
  runtime paths use 0 by default.
- metadata_creation_is_deterministic_for_same_seed (replay.rs test):
  new unit test asserting TraceMetadata::new(42) == TraceMetadata::new(42)
  and recorded_at == 0.
- replay_recording_metadata_is_stable_for_same_seed (lab/runtime.rs
  test): new integration test creating two LabRuntimes with seed 42
  and asserting their finished replay traces have identical metadata
  and events.
- metadata_new test: updated assertion to expect recorded_at == 0.

Callers that need wall-clock timestamps can set recorded_at explicitly
after construction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
